title: Moment elephant breaks out of enclosure and explores zoo grounds
description: An Asian elephant named Alice took herself on a leisurely stroll through ABQ BioPark in Albuquerque, New Mexico, after escaping her enclosure on Sunday. ABQ BioPark said  Alice was found by zoo security outside of her primary habitat.   No guests were present at the time, according to the zoo, who added that they delayed opening for 30 minutes. #elephant #zoo #escape #NewMexico #animal

title: Months ahead 'won't be easy' as Aussies urged there are 'simple ways' to stop fuel crisis | 7NEWS
description: Anthony Albanese has called on Australians to “think of others” and stop panic buying petrol as the federal government stares down months of uncertainty due to the Middle East war.   In his landmark Address to the Nation on Wednesday night, the prime minister called for calm and urged Australians to catch public transport when they can, reduce using their car where possible and only buy as much fuel as they needed.   7NEWS' special coverage on the Prime Minister's Address to the Nation was hosted by 7NEWS presenter Michael Usher alongside 7NEWS' political editor Mark Riley. Opposition leader Angus Taylor also joined to discuss the speech. title: Emu breaks up fight between two kangaroos at Australian Reptile Park
description: In a coat of arms clash, a stubborn emu has broken up a fight between two kangaroos at the Australian Reptile Park on the NSW Central Coast. When the roos began to biff, the big bird stepped in to "restore order", one witness said. #emu #kangaroo #australia #animals #aussieanimals

title: Perth servo investigated for fuel grade fraud | 7NEWS
description: A Perth petrol station, Burk Fuel, is under investigation by Consumer Protection for allegedly selling lower-grade 91 octane fuel as premium 95 or 98 octane. Owner Umar Farooq denies the allegations and claims his business is being unfairly targeted due to its competitive pricing. The state government has confirmed it is investigating the octane quality of Burk's fuel, with testing frequency increased from once every three months to every two weeks. title: Five teens arrested over Brisbane home invasion spree | 7NEWS
description: Five teenagers aged between 16 and 19 have been arrested following a series of alleged violent crimes across Brisbane and the Gold Coast. The incidents include a home invasion in Rochedale where Elena Parolin and her husband were threatened at knifepoint, an attempted carjacking in Calamvale where a woman was allegedly run over, and an attempted Mercedes-Benz theft in Robina. The teenagers were tracked down by the dog squad and are expected to face court with charges imminent. title: PM faces backlash over oil crisis address | 7NEWS
description: The Prime Minister faces criticism following a prime time address to the nation about the escalating global oil crisis. The speech encouraged Australians to remain optimistic heading into Easter, use public transport where possible, and reserve fuel for essential workers like farmers, first responders and truckers. The address comes as the West Australian Premier Roger Cook declares a state of emergency, with the Prime Minister scheduled to address the National Press Club at midday.
